title: Generic Properties of Laplace Eigenfunctions in the Presence of Symmetry
speaker: Craig Sutton
abstract: Let G be a compact Lie group acting on a closed manifold M. Partially motivated by work of Uhlenbeck (1976), we explore the generic properties of Laplace eigenfunctions associated to G-invariant metrics on M. We find that, in the case where 𝕋 is a torus acting freely on M, the Laplace eigenspaces associated to a generic 𝕋-invariant metric are irreducible representations of 𝕋.

proof_stages.label: Classical generic spectral baseline and notation
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Uhlenbeck-type generic metric results—simple spectrum, regular zero sets, and Morse eigenfunctions—and fixes notation for closed manifolds, Laplacians, nodal sets, group actions, irreducible representations, and invariant metrics.

proof_stages.label: Representation-theoretic replacement for simple spectrum
proof_stages.summary: For an isometric group action, the regular action on functions commutes with the Laplacian, so eigenspaces are group representations and the correct symmetry-compatible notion of simplicity is that each eigenspace be irreducible.

proof_stages.label: Isotypical criterion for G-simple spectrum
proof_stages.summary: Using the decomposition of L^2(M) into isotypical components, G-simple spectrum is characterized by disjoint eigenvalue sets for inequivalent components together with irreducibility of the restricted eigenspaces inside each component.

proof_stages.label: Previously known generic simplicity results and obstacles
proof_stages.summary: The speaker surveys known results for finite groups, left-invariant metrics on compact Lie groups, trivial principal bundles, and S^1-bundles, emphasizing that existing criteria are limited and that higher-dimensional irreducible representations create obstructions.

proof_stages.label: Generic T-simple spectrum for free torus actions
proof_stages.summary: The first main theorem states that for a free torus action with quotient of positive dimension, T-simple spectrum is residual among T-invariant C^l metrics, aided by the fact that real torus irreducibles have dimension one or two.

proof_stages.label: Nodal-set framework and nodal-count targets
proof_stages.summary: The talk shifts to nodal sets and domains, recalls the regular/singular decomposition for Laplace eigenfunctions and Courant-Pleijel-type constraints, and formulates the goal of obtaining generic connected nodal hypersurfaces with exactly two nodal domains under symmetry.

proof_stages.label: Generic regularity and two-domain theorem under torus symmetry
proof_stages.summary: The second main theorem gives a residual set of invariant metrics for which zero is a regular value, and under a nontriviality condition on associated circle bundles over a quotient of dimension at least two, every noninvariant eigenfunction has connected nodal set and exactly two nodal domains.

proof_stages.label: Associated circle-bundle mechanism and scope
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains how characters of the torus produce associated rank-two bundles and circle bundles whose nontriviality forces orbitwise nonvanishing behavior, derives a cohomological corollary and density-one supply of two-domain eigenfunctions, and discusses limitations involving invariant eigenfunctions, dimension, real versus complex representations, freeness, compact Lie groups, and discrete actions.
